naming_mismatches.docstring: Trace out subsystem B from rho_AB. Returns rho_A.
naming_mismatches.actual_operation: traces out A (contracts positions 0,2 = dim_a), returns rho_B
naming_mismatches.bug: NAME MISMATCH: function says 'trace B, return rho_A' but einsum traces A, returns rho_B
naming_mismatches.severity: HIGH -- callers get rho_B labeled as rho_A

naming_mismatches.docstring: Trace out subsystem A from rho_AB. Returns rho_B.
naming_mismatches.actual_operation: traces out B (contracts positions 1,3 = dim_b), returns rho_A
naming_mismatches.bug: NAME MISMATCH: function says 'trace A, return rho_B' but einsum traces B, returns rho_A
naming_mismatches.severity: HIGH -- callers get rho_A labeled as rho_B

naming_mismatches.docstring: Trace out system B from a composite rho_{AB}.
naming_mismatches.actual_operation: contracts i at positions 1,3 = dim_B, keeps j,k at 0,2 = dim_A -> returns rho_A
naming_mismatches.bug: NAME MISLEADING: function named 'partial_trace_A' but traces B. Name convention inconsistent (other files: partial_trace_A means 'trace OUT A').
naming_mismatches.severity: MEDIUM -- math is correct (returns rho_A), but naming convention opposite to rest of codebase

torch_partial_trace_A.note: Traces A (contracts 0,2), returns rho_B. Correctly named.

torch_partial_trace_B.note: Traces B (contracts 1,3), returns rho_A. Correctly named.

numpy_partial_trace_A.note: Same correct pattern as torch version.

numpy_partial_trace_B.note: Same correct pattern as torch version.

N1_random_trace_C.note: Traces qubit C from 3-qubit via reshape (4,2,4,2). Correct.

torch_partial_trace_A.note: Traces A, returns rho_B. Correctly named.

torch_partial_trace_B.note: Traces B, returns rho_A. Correctly named.

partial_trace_A_line210.note: DEAD CODE BUG: line 210 has wrong pattern 'aibj->ij' that sums a,b independently. Line 213 overwrites with correct 'aiaj->ij'. The wrong line executes but result is discarded.

partial_trace_A_line213.note: Correct pattern, overwrites the buggy line 210 result.

sim_torch_axis0_gradient.py.severity: LOW -- dead code, overwritten by correct line. But should be removed to prevent copy-paste propagation.

partial_trace_A.note: Traces A from 8x8, returns 4x4 rho_BC. Correct.

partial_trace_C.note: Traces C from 8x8, returns 4x4 rho_AB. Correct.

partial_trace_AB.note: Traces AB from 8x8, returns 2x2 rho_C. Correct.

partial_trace_BC.note: Traces BC from 8x8, returns 2x2 rho_A. Correct.

numpy_Ic_A_BC_line404.note: Traces out A, returns rho_BC. Correct.

numpy_Ic_AB_C_line411.note: Traces out AB, returns rho_C. Correct.

sim_torch_axis0_3qubit.py.note: This file was the one that CAUGHT the 'aibj' bug. All patterns here are correct.

partial_trace_A_line115.note: LIVE BUG: 'aibj->ij' sums a,b independently. Should be 'aiaj->ij'. Called by coherent_info_A_to_B() -- coherent information values are WRONG.

numpy_rho_B_line473.note: LIVE BUG: same 'aibj->ij' error. Bloch vector for B will be wrong.

sim_axis0_through_shells.py.severity: CRITICAL -- coherent information and Bloch vectors computed with wrong partial trace. All results from this file are suspect.

partial_trace_B_numpy.note: Manual loop: rho_A[i,j] = sum_k rho[2*i+k, 2*j+k]. Correct for trace B.

partial_trace_A_numpy.note: Manual loop: rho_B[k,l] = sum_i rho[2*i+k, 2*i+l]. Correct for trace A.

torch_partial_trace_B.note: Same manual loop as numpy version, differentiable. Correct.

sim_torch_cnot.py.note: Uses explicit loops, no einsum. All implementations correct.

partial_trace_b.note: Einsum traces A (correct math), but function named 'trace_b' and docstring says 'returns rho_A'. Actually returns rho_B. Callers get SWAPPED subsystems.

partial_trace_a.note: Einsum traces B (correct math), but function named 'trace_a' and docstring says 'returns rho_B'. Actually returns rho_A. Callers get SWAPPED subsystems.

sim_torch_ratchet_gnn.py.caller_impact: Line 549-550: rho_a_out = partial_trace_b(rho_ab) actually gets rho_B. SWAPPED.
sim_torch_ratchet_gnn.py.severity: HIGH -- subsystem labels swapped at call sites

partial_trace_A.note: Traces A, returns rho_B. Correctly named.

partial_trace_B.note: Traces B, returns rho_A. Correctly named.

np_partial_trace_B.note: Traces out B (contracts dim_b at positions 1,3). Returns rho_A. Correct.

np_partial_trace_A.note: Traces out A (contracts dim_a at positions 0,2). Returns rho_B. Correct.

torch_partial_trace_B.note: MISMATCH: when which='B' (trace out B), uses 'ijik->jk' which traces OUT A. Returns rho_B instead of rho_A. Torch and numpy disagree!

torch_partial_trace_A.note: MISMATCH: when which='A' (trace out A), uses 'ijkj->ik' which traces OUT B. Returns rho_A instead of rho_B. Torch and numpy disagree!

sim_phase7_divergence_analysis.py.severity: HIGH -- torch version swaps which subsystem is traced vs numpy version. If results are compared torch-vs-numpy, divergence analysis is comparing wrong subsystems.

partial_trace_A.note: Function 'partial_trace_A' with doc 'trace out B' -- returns rho_A. Math is correct but naming convention is opposite (elsewhere partial_trace_A means 'trace OUT A').

critical_bugs.fix: Replace 'aibj->ij' with 'aiaj->ij' at both lines. All results from this file are invalid.
Remove line 210 (dead code, overwritten by correct line 213)
Swap function names: partial_trace_b should use 'ijkj->ik' (trace B), partial_trace_a should use 'ijik->jk' (trace A)

critical_bugs.pattern: torch vs numpy disagreement on which subsystem traced
critical_bugs.fix: Swap einsum patterns in torch_partial_trace to match numpy: which='B' should use 'ijkj->ik', which='A' should use 'ijik->jk'
Rename to partial_trace_B or trace_out_B to match codebase convention

summary.verdict: 4 files need fixes. 1 CRITICAL live bug (sim_axis0_through_shells.py), 1 dead code removal, 2 naming/swap bugs that affect correctness.
